# FPF-Spec.md upstream changes 2026-04-20 → 2026-05-16

## Quantitative delta

|---|---|---|---|
| LOC | 62 202 | 72 800 | +10 598 (+17.0%) |
| File size (bytes) | 5 613 613 | ~6.3M | +12% |

## Header-level diff
Section headings (## / ###) are largely unchanged in name and ordering through
Part A kernel and Part B reasoning. Insertions concentrate inside existing
sections (more sub-bullets, more SoTA-Echoing examples, more
Anti-Patterns blocks, more (F, ClaimScope, R)-annotated examples).

## E.10.SEMIO campaign (commit subject)
The upstream HEAD commit message names a "corrections for E.10.SEMIO campaign"
sweep. E.10.SEMIO refers to FPF Part E §E.10 — the SEMIO (semiotic) family of
extensions covering inscription / language-act / boundary-statement rules.
Concrete corrections present in the upstream that were NOT in the 2026-04-20
vendored snapshot:

1. **A.6.P** — new sub-pattern under A.6 role-coordination block. Adds a
   "publication discipline" sub-clause linking SEMIO boundary statements to
   role-output artefacts (deeper integration of Part A + Part E).
2. **E.10.SEMIO** — clarifications around when inscription is normative vs
   informative; new conformance items (CC‑E10.\*) and rationale blocks
   citing post-2015 corroboration.
3. **Cross-cluster Relations refresh** — Several `### <Module>:12 - Relations`
   blocks updated to cite the new E.10.SEMIO patterns.

## What this means for the Jetix distillation

The 01-fpf-on-human-language.md (Phase A) was based on the 2026-04-20
vendored snapshot. The Phase B v2 tightening:

- Adds an explicit pointer to E.10.SEMIO as the inscription-discipline anchor
  (relevant for Jetix's append-only logs, F-G-R-tagged claims, prose
  authorship discipline — Pillar C Tier 2 R6).
- Adds the A.6.P role-publication-discipline reference (relevant for our
  Foundation Part 4 Role Taxonomy + Part 11 Strategic Direction).
- Notes that FPF is a moving target — the canonical snapshot is now
  `2026-05-16 / c86eabd`.
